text: The arrow identified the median sacral artery that perfused the aneurysm sac in a retrograde fashion.
It was felt that ligating this artery would adequately manage the endoleak and the patient was brought to the operating room for laparoscopic median sacral artery ligation.
This trocar was eventually changed for a 12 mm trocar in order to clip the artery.
Finally, a third 5 mm trocar was inserted at the left iliac fossa.
The right iliac artery was identified.
The peritoneum was opened medially to the right iliac artery using the electrocuttery.
Previous imaging showed this artery to be thrombosed.
The dissection began under the superior mesorectum.
The dissection was continued distally towards the sacrum.
Gentle blunt dissection was used to identify the left iliac vein.
This was our anatomical landmark as we know that the median sacral artery course under this vein.
The median sacral artery was then identified over the sacrum and isolated using blunt dissection.
Here you can see the median sacral artery coursing under the left iliac vein.
This view confirms the anatomical landmarks and that the median sacral artery was correctly identified.
The 5 mm trocar situated at the right flank was changed for a 12 mm trocar.
The Maryland dissector was used to complete circumferential dissection of the artery for clipping.
Two metallic clips were used to ligate the artery.
